Name: IDC Agri-Industrial Bond 2022A (ZAR 2.5 billion) – JSE Listed
Description: 10-year fixed-rate bond issued by the Industrial Development Corporation under its ZAR 20B Domestic Medium-Term Note Programme. Coupon 9.0% p.a. Proceeds fund IDC agri-industrial loans: agri-processing, food & beverage, animal feed, frozen fish and aquaculture value chains. JSE listing ref JSE/IDC/2022/AGR/001. NT guarantee. S&P: AA(za) National Scale.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Manufacturing Finance Bond 2023A (ZAR 3.8 billion) – JSE Listed
Description: 12-year bond, Coupon 9.5% p.a. DMTN Programme. Proceeds on-lent to South African manufacturing enterprises in automotive, chemicals, plastics, metals, textiles and electronics. IDC mandate: industrial diversification and employment creation. 45,000 jobs supported. JSE listing ref JSE/IDC/2023/MFG/001. NT guarantee. Moody's: Baa2.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Mining Finance Bond 2023B (ZAR 4.2 billion) – JSE Listed
Description: 8-year mining sector bond, Coupon 10.25% p.a. Proceeds finance IDC loans to mining companies for expansion, beneficiation and mine rehabilitation. Major loan book: Anglo American Platinum, Exxaro, Kumba Iron Ore, Northam Platinum and Harmony Gold. JSE listing ref JSE/IDC/2023/MIN/002. NT guarantee. S&P AA(za).
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Green Economy Bond 2024A (ZAR 1.9 billion) – CBI Certified
Description: 7-year green bond aligned with CBI Green Bond Standard – Renewable Energy, Energy Efficiency and Clean Transport categories. Coupon 8.75% p.a. Proceeds fund IDC investments in solar, wind, green hydrogen, electric vehicles and energy efficiency retrofits. JSE Sustainability Segment listed. Sustainalytics second-party opinion: 86/100. NT approval.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Township Economy Bond 2024B (ZAR 1.1 billion) – Social Bond
Description: 5-year social bond targeting township economy revitalisation and BBBEE enterprise development. Coupon 9.25% p.a. Proceeds finance IDC township manufacturing, spaza formalisation, township retail centres and agri-hubs. ICMA Social Bond Principles aligned. Targets 12,000 jobs in 8 townships. JSE Sustainability Segment. NT approval. DTIC endorsement.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Equity – Impala Platinum Holdings (4.8% strategic stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing IDC's 4.8% strategic stake in Impala Platinum Holdings Ltd (Implats), valued at JSE market price (ZAR 48.2/share × 62M IDC shares). Implats total attributable PGM production: 1.48 Moz 6E (FY2024). IDC board representation: 1 non-executive director. Dividend received FY2024: ZAR 280M. IDC portfolio ref IDC-IMPLATS-2024.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Equity – PPC Cement Holdings (10.4% strategic stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing IDC's 10.4% strategic stake in PPC Ltd, Africa's leading cement manufacturer. Valued at JSE price (ZAR 4.85/share × 123M IDC shares). PPC capacity: 11.5 Mta across South Africa, Zimbabwe, Rwanda, Ethiopia and DRC. IDC non-executive board seat. Annual dividend: ZAR 62M. PPC portfolio supports infrastructure and construction SDGs.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Equity – Sasol HySA Green Hydrogen JV (35% stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing IDC's 35% strategic stake in the Sasol–IDC–DTIC Hydrogen South Africa (HySA) Phase 1 JV at Richards Bay. Project: 100 MW green electrolyser powered by 220 MW wind farm (Siemens Gamesa). Green hydrogen offtake for Sasol gas-to-liquids process. Capital cost: ZAR 6.8B. IDC committed equity: ZAR 1.2B. DTIC Green Hydrogen Strategy alignment.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Equity – ArcelorMittal SA Strategic Stake (19.7%)
Description: Tokenised equity representing IDC's 19.7% strategic stake in ArcelorMittal South Africa (AMSA), operator of the Vanderbijlpark and Newcastle steel mills. AMSA annual production: 4.2 Mta steel. IDC equity: 198M shares at JSE price (ZAR 5.2/share = ZAR 1.03B). IDC provides strategic oversight and industrial policy alignment for domestic steel security.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Equity – Massmart Township Economy Fund (24% stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing IDC's 24% strategic stake in the Massmart–IDC Township Economy Fund – a ZAR 2B enterprise and supplier development initiative supporting 1,800 township SMME suppliers to the Massmart/Walmart supply chain in food, FMCG and general merchandise. IDC equity: ZAR 480M. DTIC BBBEE s.10 enterprise development incentive.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC–World Bank IBRD On-Lending Facility (USD 500 million)
Description: IBRD on-lending facility channelled through NT/IDC for industrial finance lending to South African manufacturing SMEs and large enterprises. World Bank project P178234B. 20-year term, 5-year grace, IBRD fixed rate + IDC margin. Focus: auto components, agri-processing, green manufacturing, beneficiation. NT sovereign guarantee. Annual World Bank portfolio review.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC–China Development Bank Credit Line (USD 300 million)
Description: Long-term credit line from China Development Bank to IDC for financing industrial collaboration projects under SA-China bilateral economic agreement. CDB loan ref CDB/RSA/IDC/2022/001. 15-year term, 5% all-in rate. Committed to: Saldanha Bay steel hub, KwaZulu-Natal industrial corridor and BRICS+ cross-border trade finance for SA exporters.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC–EIB Green Economy Finance Facility (EUR 400 million)
Description: European Investment Bank facility to IDC for on-lending to South African green economy businesses: renewable energy, energy efficiency, clean transport, sustainable agri and circular economy. EIB agreement EIB/RSA/IDC/2024/001. 20-year term, EIB benchmark + 1.0% margin. First disbursement: EUR 120M to REIPPP and green hydrogen projects. NT guarantee.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC NT Government-Backed Guarantee (ZAR 35 billion portfolio coverage)
Description: Government of South Africa backstop guarantee covering IDC's ZAR 35B domestic capital market borrowing portfolio. Issued by NT under PFMA s.70 and IDC Act No. 22 of 1940. Annual NT compact review. Enables IDC's domestic investment-grade ratings (S&P AA(za), Moody's Baa2). IDC supports 230,000 jobs annually through its loan book of ZAR 120B assets.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC SA Green Hydrogen PPP – Boegoebaai Export Terminal (ZAR 12 billion)
Description: PPP token for the SA Green Hydrogen Export Hub at Boegoebaai, Northern Cape. IDC co-developer with ACWA Power, Sasol and Anglo American. Phase 1: 1 GW electrolyser capacity, 250,000 t/yr green ammonia production for EU export. NT/DTIC/DSI approval. GreenH2 corridor alignment. IFC B-Loan: USD 400M. EU H2 Global co-financing: EUR 200M. NERSA licence pending.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Limpopo Citrus Valley Agri-Processing PPP (ZAR 2.8 billion)
Description: PPP token for the IDC–DALRRD Limpopo Citrus Valley Agri-Processing Hub: juice concentrate plant (350,000 t/yr citrus input), cold chain (15,000-pallet packhouse), export terminal (Port of Durban) and 8,000-ha smallholder citrus out-grower scheme. Private co-developer: CHB Fresh Produce and Capespan. DALRRD CASP funding: ZAR 480M. DTIC SEZ incentive.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Mpumalanga Coal Beneficiation & Carbon Offset PPP (ZAR 4.5 billion)
Description: PPP token for the IDC–Mpumalanga economic diversification programme: coal beneficiation plant upgrading 8 Mtpa thermal coal to metallurgical coal (premium export), combined with 200 MW wind farm to offset 600,000 tCO2/yr. Private co-developer: Exxaro Resources, BHP. IDC equity: ZAR 1.5B. JET-IP aligned. DFFE environmental authorisation issued EA/MP/COAL/2024/001.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Tembisa Township Manufacturing Hub PPP (ZAR 1.6 billion)
Description: PPP token for the IDC–EDD Tembisa Special Economic Zone: 85,000 sqm township manufacturing cluster targeting food processing, garment manufacturing, automotive components and electronics assembly. Private anchor tenant: Tiger Brands and Nando's supply chain. IDC equity: ZAR 520M. SEDA SME incubator (800 enterprises). 14,000 permanent jobs target. DTIC SEZ designation pending.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Saldanha Bay Industrial Development Zone – 25-Year Steel Concession
Description: Tokenised 25-year industrial concession right for IDC's operation of the Saldanha Bay Industrial Development Zone (IDZ) hot-rolled coil and pelletising facility (capacity: 1.5 Mta). IDC-led JV with ArcelorMittal SA and Kumba Iron Ore. Concession from DTIC under SEZ Act No. 16 of 2014. Annual revenue: ZAR 2.8B from steel and export pellets. IDC concession ref IDZ/SBZ/2020.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Boegoebaai Green Hydrogen Export Terminal Concession – 30 Years
Description: Tokenised 30-year port infrastructure and export terminal concession at Boegoebaai, Northern Cape (new deep-water port under development). Concession granted by TNPA/DTIC for construction and operation of green ammonia and hydrogen export facilities (jetty, storage, loading arms). Annual throughput target: 300,000 t green ammonia from Year 4. TNPA concession ref TNPA/BGI/2024.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Richards Bay Minerals Processing Concession (20-Year)
Description: Tokenised 20-year minerals beneficiation concession at Richards Bay Industrial Zone: ilmenite smelting to titania slag and titanium metal, zircon purification and synthetic rutile processing. Concession granted to IDC–Rio Tinto JV by DTIC under SEZ Act. Annual production: 240,000 t titania slag, 80,000 t zircon. DMRE mining right ref DMRE/RBM/2022. Revenue: ZAR 3.4B/yr.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Coega SEZ Industrial Land Concession – Phase 5 (1,200 ha)
Description: Tokenised 50-year industrial land concession for IDC's anchor tenancy and co-development rights at Coega Special Economic Zone Phase 5 (1,200 ha, Eastern Cape). IDC has developed 28 industrial stands with anchor clients in auto components, agri-processing, chemicals and logistics. DTIC SEZ designation under SEZ Act. Annual land and infrastructure levy revenue: ZAR 480M. CDC ref COEGA/IDC/2024.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Secunda Beneficiation Plant Revenue Bond (ZAR 3.2 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by Sasol-Secunda coal beneficiation facility processing fees and petrochemical feedstock revenues. IDC co-financed Sasol's R8B CTL-to-gas facility retrofit at Secunda (reducing coal input by 40%). Annual processing fee income to IDC: ZAR 680M. DSCR: 1.52×. JET-IP aligned. SECZ ref SEC/RSA/IRB/2023/045. NT guarantee.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Green Energy Revenue Bond – Wind Farm Portfolio (ZAR 2.4 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by revenues from IDC's 15-project wind energy portfolio (combined 1,140 MW across Northern and Western Cape): Eskom PPA and wheeling revenues. Annual revenue: ZAR 520M. DSCR: 1.68×. CBI Green Bond aligned. IFC B-Loan co-financed: USD 120M. NT guarantee. JSE Sustainability Segment. SECZ ref SEC/RSA/IRB/2024/012.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)

Name: IDC Eastern Cape Agri-Processing Revenue Bond (ZAR 1.8 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by agri-processing plant lease and offtake revenues in Eastern Cape IDC-funded facilities: chicory processing (750,000 t/yr), rooibos extract (800 t/yr), wool scouring (18,000 t/yr). Annual combined revenue: ZAR 420M. DSCR: 1.45×. AfDB/IFAD co-financing: USD 35M. DALRRD agri-park support. NT guarantee. SECZ ref SEC/RSA/IRB/2024/028.
OwnerName: Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC)
